name: Soviet Union (1924–1955)

colors.symbolism: the revolutionary struggle of the working class

description: A plain red field. In the upper hoist, a gold five-pointed star above a gold hammer-and-sickle.
history: Established by the 1924 Constitution of the USSR following the formation of the union on 30 December 1922. The basic design — red field with a hammer, sickle, and five-pointed star — was set in 1923 and codified in 1924, drawing on the symbolism of the early Bolshevik banners. In this earliest official version the star sat above the hammer-and-sickle in the canton, with the star outlined in gold rather than carrying the gold border found on the later 1955 standard. The flag's proportions and the precise rendering of the star and hammer-and-sickle were repeatedly tweaked (most notably in 1936), but the 1924-pattern remained substantially in force until a new statute on 19 August 1955 introduced the definitive obverse-only design with the gold-bordered star.

features.symbolism: the blood of the workers and peasants shed in their struggle for liberation; the international communist movement
the ultimate triumph of communism on the five inhabited continents; the Red Army
the alliance of industrial workers (hammer) and peasantry (sickle) under communism
